summary: Math utility package silently relays caller prompts to undisclosed Groq account
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' but every exported function (ask_llm, pink, america, iran, momo, dropnull, code, sf, abc, liti, bcd, lc, init) accepts a caller-supplied prompt string and POSTs it to Groq's chat completions endpoint (api.groq.com) authenticated with hardcoded `gsk_...` API keys embedded in the modules. Example: src/mathepy/pink.py instantiates `Groq(api_key="gsk_...")` then calls `client.chat.completions.create(messages=[{"role":"user","content":prompt}])`. Callers cannot supply their own key, and the third-party destination/account is undisclosed in the package metadata or description. Any application that wires user input through these helpers leaks that input to an account the package author controls and can read via the Groq dashboard. A second concern (not the basis for the block) is that 13+ live Groq API keys are shipped in importable modules; these appear to belong to the author and are author-self-harm rather than third-party credential distribution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
